name: AI Feature Add-on
summary: One AI feature added to your existing SaaS. Classification, summarization, structured extraction, or a single agent behind a chat surface.
forWho: Solo SaaS founder or 1–10 person team with a live product that needs one AI capability shipped cleanly.
scope: One feature end-to-end — prompt design, eval set, API integration, UI plumbing
Anthropic, OpenAI, or open-source model depending on fit
Basic observability: logs, usage tracking, failure alerts
Code in your repo, full handoff, no lock-in

name: Agentic MVP
summary: A working agentic system from zero — one agent, a handful of tools, real data, a usable UI. Enough to test the idea with users and decide whether to invest further.
forWho: Founders validating an agentic product idea. You need something real in front of users, not a prototype that dies in your laptop.
scope: Agent architecture + tool design (typically 3–6 tools)
Eval set for the core task, with tracked quality metrics
Production deployment on Cloudflare, Vercel, or your cloud
Usable web UI — not a Streamlit demo
Weekly Loom + code demo every Friday

name: Custom (Tier B)
summary: Your project doesn't fit the two above but is still in the $8–15K range. We scope on the call, then I write a fixed SOW.
forWho: Projects that need a different shape — a research spike, a narrow rebuild, a short integration engagement.
scope: Defined on the scoping call, fixed in writing before work starts
outOfScope: Anything not in the signed SOW

name: Agentic Workflow
summary: Production agentic workflow inside your existing product or ops. Multi-step agent, tool use, eval-driven improvements, real deployment.
forWho: Seed–Series B teams with a product and revenue. You know what you want the agent to do — I build and ship it.
scope: Multi-step agent with 5–10 tools
Eval suite covering primary tasks + regression set
Observability, cost tracking, rate-limit handling
Production deployment to your infra
Weekly Loom demos, complete handoff
outOfScope: Custom model fine-tuning (happy to recommend when it's the right call)

name: Agentic Platform
summary: Multiple agents, shared tools, memory, routing, and a usable admin surface. The backbone of an agentic product — not one feature, a platform.
forWho: Teams building a product where agents are the core value, not a side feature.
scope: Multi-agent architecture with router + shared memory layer
Reusable tool library with permission scoping
Admin surface for tuning prompts, tools, evals without shipping code
Full eval pipeline + regression tests in CI
Production deployment + observability stack
outOfScope: Data warehouse builds — I'll integrate with your existing one

name: Custom Agentic System
summary: Work that doesn't fit the two above — domain-specific, higher complexity, longer horizon. I bring in a vetted ML or DevOps contractor if the scope genuinely needs it.
forWho: Teams with an ambitious agentic bet that justifies a longer engagement. I'll only take this if I can actually ship it well.
scope: Defined on the scoping call and fixed in a written SOW
outOfScope: Anything not in the signed SOW
